# Data Engineer

Industry🚰 Data Engineer

### Job Description
[...]
Data Engineer developing data solutions for climate and energy at Carrier Global Corporation. Building and maintaining data pipelines and models to enable analytics and reporting.

#### Responsibilities:
[...]
- Build and maintain data ingestion, transformation, and delivery pipelines that support batch and micro‑batch workloads.

[...]
- Contribute to the design of data models across raw, refined, and curated layers.
[...]
- Apply medallion architecture principles to support data quality, consistency, and reusability.
[...]
- Support integration of ERP and enterprise systems by preparing, transforming, and harmonizing source data.
[...]
- Participate in discussions on performance, scalability, and data design decisions.
[...]

- Bachelor’s Degree.
- 3+ years of experience as a data engineer, with demonstrated hands‑on delivery of data pipelines and models

results.title: Carrier BI Engineer 2026: Analytics Job Openings in Hyderabad
highlights:
# Carrier Hiring BI Engineer 2026: Analytics Job Openings for Experienced Professionals
[...]
Carrier Global Corporation, a global leader in intelligent climate and energy solutions, is seeking a skilled Business Intelligence (BI) Engineer for its Hyderabad office. This role is critical in transforming raw data into actionable insights, driving data-informed decisions across the organization. As Carrier continues its digital transformation journey, the BI Engineer will be at the forefront of designing scalable analytics solutions, integrating AI-driven features like Microsoft Copilot, and supporting business growth through advanced reporting and dashboards. The position offers a unique opportunity to work with modern tech stacks including Snowflake, Databricks, and Power BI, making it a compelling career move for analytics professionals aiming to impact a Fortune 500 company.
[...]
| Company | Carrier |
| --- | --- |
| Role | Business Intelligence (BI) Engineer Analytics |
| Location | Hyderabad, India |
| Experience | 3-5 Years |
| Education | Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, IT, or related field |
| Salary | ₹8-12 LPA (estimated based on industry standards for 3-5 years experience) |
[...]
Candidates must hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a closely related field. The role requires 3–5 years of hands-on experience in BI development or data engineering, with expert-level SQL skills and proficiency in Power BI (including DAX and Power Query) or Tableau. Strong database knowledge (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks) is mandatory, along with familiarity in Python or R for advanced analytics. Preferred qualifications include the Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate certification. Candidates with a background in financial metrics and business logic understanding will have a distinct advantage. While the role is not for freshers, early-career professionals with relevant internship or project experience demonstrating equivalent skills may be considered.
[...]
- Implement AI-driven features like Microsoft Copilot for natural language querying and automated narratives.
[...]
Carrier is investing heavily in digital transformation, and this BI Engineer role is a gateway to working with cutting-edge tools like Snowflake, Databricks, and AI copilots. Unlike generic analytics positions, Carrier expects you to bridge the gap between technical data engineering and business strategy. With 3–5 years of experience, you’re not just building dashboards—you’re shaping how the company measures success. The emphasis on financial metrics suggests exposure to high-impact domains like supply chain, sales, and operations analytics. For professionals aiming to move into senior data engineering or analytics leadership, this role provides the perfect blend of technical depth and strategic visibility.
[...]
One of the most forward-looking aspects of this job description is the mention of AI integration via Microsoft Copilot. In 2026, companies like Carrier are pushing beyond traditional BI by embedding natural language querying and automated insights into dashboards. As a BI Engineer, you’ll need to understand how to configure Copilot within Power BI, manage data sources for AI readiness, and ensure output accuracy. This is not a standalone AI role—it’s about augmenting your existing SQL and visualization skills with AI fluency. If you’re already comfortable with Python or R, you’re ahead of the curve. This trend means the role is future-proofed, and your learning curve will directly correlate with career growth.
[...]
’s degree in Computer Science,
[...]
Tableau skills
[...]
the link provided in the job description
[...]
resume highlights relevant experience in SQL, Power BI, ETL, and data modeling
[...]

results.title: Specialist, Customer Data Management | Hirebase
highlights: corporate.carrier.com or follow Carrier on social media at @Carrier. About this role: Carrier is seeking a Product Owner / Data Analyst to support the Data Center IPMT business group. This hybrid role blends product ownership, business analysis, and hands‑on analytics to deliver rapid insights, scalable KPIs, and data‑driven decision support. The successful candidate will serve as the primary analytics intake and prioritization point for IPMT, translating business needs into actionable analytics products while partnering closely with Digital, Finance, and Governance teams to ensure clarity, consistency, and adoption. Key Responsibilities: Serve as the primary analytics point of contact for the Data Center IPMT business, owning intake, prioritization, and backlog management for analytics work. Translate business questions, requests, and improvement opportunities into clear user stories, acceptance criteria, and prioritized deliverables. Maintain an analytics roadmap aligned to IPMT priorities, dependencies, and Digital funding models. Perform rapid, high‑impact analysis using existing datasets, dashboards, and tools to deliver timely insights. Develop, enhance, and maintain KPIs, dashboards, and reporting that provide transparency into business performance. Storytelling insights through executive‑ready presentations, dashboards, and written narratives to drive informed decisions. Serve as the business subject matter expert for KPIs, definitions, and data usage within IPMT. Partner with CHVAC Data Analytics to provide KPI definition inputs, enforce change control, and support documentation standards. Collaborate with CHVAC Data Analytics and business teams to distinguish net‑new development vs. enhancement of existing funded solutions. Identify when requests require deeper data engineering or structural changes and route through agreed intake processes. Support adoption of analytics products through stakeholder engagement, enablement, and ongoing support. Required Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree 5 + years of experience in data analytics, business analytics, and/or product‑adjacent analytics roles. 5 + years of hands‑on experience with Power BI, Excel, and PowerPoint Preferred Qualifications: Proven ability to translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical work and actionable insights. Strong communication and storytelling skills, including experience presenting to business and leadership audiences. Experience working with CRM, ERP, or operational data sources. Strong organizational, prioritization, and stakeholder management skills. Preferred Qualifications Experience in a Product Owner, Analytics Product Owner, or hybrid PO/Data Analyst role. Familiarity with Agile delivery concepts, backlog management, and user story development. Experience with SQL, Power Automate, Power Apps, or similar analytics enablement tools. Background supporting manufacturing, capital projects, or data center–related operations. Experience contributing to KPI governance, standardization, or analytics operating mode Additional information: Must have unrestricted authorization to work in the USA. No visa sponsoring available Benefits: Employees are eligible for benefits, including: Health Care benefits: Medical, Dental, Vision; wellness incentives Retirement benefits Time Off and Leave: Paid vacation days, up to 15 days; paid sick days, up to 5 days; paid personal leave, up to 5 days; paid holidays, up to 13 days; birth and adoption leave; parental leave; family and medical leave; bereavement leave; jury duty; military leave; purchased vacation Disability: Short-term and long-term disability Life Insurance and Accidental Death and Dismemberment Tax-Advantaged Accounts: Health Savings Account; Healthcare Spending Account; Dependent Care Spending Account Tuition Assistance To learn more about our benefits offering, please click here: Work With Us | Carrier Corporate The specific benefits available to any employee

results.title: Application Engineer Modernization | Zenvok
highlights: Innovationen zum Nutzen unserer Kunden vorantreibt. Besuchen Sie unsere Website: https://www.carrier.com/commercial/de/de/ Abteilung Service-Modernisierung Die Abteilung Modernisierung verantwortet die technische und konzeptionelle Weiterentwicklung von Bestandsanlagen mit dem Ziel, diese energetisch zu optimieren, betriebssicher zu machen und nachhaltig für zukünftige Anforderungen aufzustellen. Im Fokus stehen Effizienzsteigerung, Dekarbonisierung sowie die Integration moderner Technologien und Systeme in bestehende Anlagenstrukturen. Ihre Aufgaben Technische Schnittstelle zwischen Vertrieb und Projektabwicklung in der Projektierungs- und Angebotsphase sowie während der Abwicklung Unterstützung der Angebotserstellung durch verfahrenstechnische Auslegungen und technische Konzepte Erstellung projektspezifischer technischer Dokumentationen (z. B. Funktionsbeschreibungen, Prozessbeschreibungen, technische Spezifikationen) Auslegung und Optimierung von Kälte- und Wärmeanlagen im HVAC-Bereich unter Berücksichtigung energetischer und wirtschaftlicher Aspekte Analyse von Bestandsanlagen inklusive Identifikation von Schwachstellen sowie Entwicklung geeigneter Optimierungsmaßnahmen Erstellung von R&I-Schemata sowie Unterstützung bei der technischen Planung (CAD-Kenntnisse von Vorteil) Entwicklung energieeffizienter und zukunftssicherer Anlagenkonzepte im Rahmen von Modernisierungsprojekten Aufbau, Pflege und Weiterentwicklung eines Netzwerks von Fachunterlieferanten im Engineering-Bereich Fachliche Führung von Unterlieferanten im Engineering sowie aktives Tracking des Projektfortschritts und Sicherstellung der termingerechten Zuarbeit Technische Abstimmung mit internen und externen Projektbeteiligten Ihr Profil Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Verfahrenstechnik, Versorgungstechnik, Maschinenbau oder alternativ eine Weiterbildung zum Techniker bzw. Meister im Bereich Klima- und Kältetechnik mit entsprechender Praxiserfahrung Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in der Auslegung von Kälte- und Wärmeanlagen im HVAC-Umfeld Fundierte Kenntnisse in der Hydraulik von Rohrleitungssystemen (Kälte- und Wärmeanwendungen) Erfahrung in der Erstellung von R&I-Fließbildern sowie im Umgang mit CAD-Systemen (wünschenswert) Fähigkeit zur Erstellung von 3D-Konzeptmodellen (z. B. zur Visualisierung von Anlagenlayouts und Systemintegration) mithilfe gängiger CAD Systeme Sehr gutes Verständnis für verfahrenstechnische Zusammenhänge und Prozesse Fähigkeit zur strukturierten Analyse von Bestandsanlagen und Ableitung technischer Optimierungsmaßnahmen Erfahrung in der Erstellung technischer Dokumentationen und Beschreibungen Selbstständige, strukturierte Arbeitsweise sowie ausgeprägte Schnittstellenkompetenz Ziel der Position Als Application Engineer in der Modernisierung tragen Sie maßgeblich dazu bei, bestehende Anlagen technisch, energetisch und wirtschaftlich auf ein zukunftsfähiges Niveau zu heben. Sie verbinden technische Expertise mit Projektverständnis und sorgen für eine reibungslose Abstimmung zwischen Vertrieb, Engineering und Abwicklung. Was die Position besonders macht Direkter Einfluss auf nachhaltige und energieeffiziente Anlagenkonzepte Hohe technische Verantwortung in anspruchsvollen Modernisierungsprojekten Schnittstellenfunktion mit großem Gestaltungsspielraum Möglichkeit, aktiv an der Weiterentwicklung von Engineering-Standards und Partnernetzwerken mitzuwirken Die Position ist als Remote-Tätigkeit deutschlandweit ausgelegt (mobiles Arbeiten mit projektbezogener Abstimmung und ggf. Vor-Ort-Terminen) Wir bieten Ein dynamisches, hilfsbereites Team Vielfältige Aufgaben in einem wachsenden Servicebereich Möglichkeiten zur Weiterbildung und persönlichen Entwicklung Moderne IT- und Systemlandschaft zur optimalen Aufgabenerfüllung Flexible Arbeitsbedingungen und attraktive Sozialleistungen Unser Engagement für Sie Unser größtes Kapital sind Fachwissen, Kreativität und die Leidenschaft unserer Mitarbeitenden. results.title: Sr Engr Cslt-AI Science, Madhapur, India | Verizon Careers
highlights:
Sr Engr Cslt-AI Science, Madhapur, India | Verizon Careers
[...]
# Sr Engr Cslt-AI Science
[...]
- Hyderabad, India
- Chennai, India
[...]
As a Senior Engineer Consultant in AI Science, you will be a technical expert and hands-on developer responsible for building, deploying, and managing end-to-end AI services to power next-generation generative and Agentic AI use cases for network autonomy. You will play a crucial role in delivering the core Autonomous Networks framework, which transforms network operations from reactive queues into a continuous, 24/7 autonomous workforce. This role requires expertise in designing and optimizing large-scale AI/ML solutions, focusing on the technical components that detect, aggregate, synthesize, investigate, and recommend actionable strategies across the global network.
[...]
Design, develop, and optimize Artificial Intelligence, SLM/LLM solutions to diverse real-world problems and implement cutting-edge generative artificial intelligence models and systems.
[...]
Implement and analyze the performance of advanced algorithms, including those from deep learning and generative AI.
[...]
Apply expertise to the five layers of autonomous agents, developing models for continuous monitoring, impact aggregation, contextual event clustering, and evidence-based root cause hypothesis generation.
[...]

highlights:
Sr AI Data Engineer in Remote, Georgia, United States of America | Digital Technology / IT at GE Aerospace Job1

GE Aerospace uses enhanced security screening measures to detect and prevent against fraudulent hires.

About Us

At GE Aerospace, we invent the future of flight, lift people up, and bring them home safely. We are a world-leading provider of jet engines, components, and integrated systems for commercial and military aircraft. At GE Aerospace, respect for people is core to our culture and commitment to FLIGHT DECK, our proprietary lean operating model. We attract, develop, and retain industry-leading talent from all backgrounds and cultivate communities where people are respected and empowered to reach their fullest potential. For the people of GE Aerospace, their work isn't just a job. It's their passion and purpose. We work and win as one team, accountable to each other and our customers, and we are always finding a better way to climb higher together. We were meant to fly.

At GE Aerospace, we value authenticity and genuine interactions during the hiring process. To ensure a fair and transparent evaluation, the use of AI tools to aid in responses, including but not limited to AI-generated answers, real-time assistance, or automated decision-making, is not allowed. Candidates are expected to engage directly and authentically, therefore, unauthorized use of AI tools during interviews may result in disqualification from the hiring process. Note: this restriction does not apply to an individual’s use of AI or technology if required for accessibility or accommodation purposes. Thank you for your understanding and cooperation. We look forward to connecting with you!

# Data and AI Engineer
[...]
Verizon’s Exposure and Vulnerability Management (EVM) team is seeking a Data & AI Engineer to lead the development, implementation, and intelligent automation of our security data system. In this role, you will be the hands-on driving force building scalable data pipelines, Creating Data Dashboards, Reports and developing Artificial Intelligence (AI) models to predict, prioritize, cybersecurity vulnerabilities.
[...]
While your primary focus will be engineering robust data and AI Reporting systems, strong architectural capabilities are a major plus. You will have the opportunity to influence the blueprint of how diverse asset data is structured and meaningfully delivered.
[...]
By engineering data models and AI pipelines, the role will help ensure our infrastructure is ready for predictive analytics and that our reporting solutions provide proactive, AI-driven insights rather than just historical data.
[...]
Data Modeling & Architecture: Designing and implementing comprehensive data models (conceptual, logical, physical) that normalize and dimensionalize complex security data across numerous sources, optimized for both BI and AI workloads.

You are a builder with a vision—someone who views data through the lens of security risk and creating ways to automate complex problems. You should be deeply analytical, thrilled by optimizing massive datasets, and eager to leverage AI to stay ahead of emerging threats. You flourish in fast-paced environments and possess the technical credibility to guide both engineering execution and architectural direction.

security frameworks (NIST, SOX, FISMA) and the full IT stack (OS, Network, Cloud, SDLC).

As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.

# Senior Manager, Artificial Intelligence Data Engineer
[...]
Global Development is embarking on a Digital Transformation project incorporating AI, machine learning, and automation to help us reduce cycle times, improve quality allowing us to focus on more meaningful work. We focus on developing and improving data pipelines, infrastructure, architecture, and analytic tools to allow resources to fuel our transformation. Working with a team of engineers, analysts, and scientists, you will contribute to modernizing our clinical data infrastructure. This highly visible role will be a technical and strategic liaison between the transformation projects and IT.
[...]
A key responsibility will be developing and optimizing schema and data models for clinical data. We ensure that those products are standard compliant and interoperable with other platforms and data sources. This role will also contribute to data governance strategy and to the development and management of tools improving and monitoring data quality. The data engineer will also develop processes to automate routine workflows. The engineer will also provide technical leadership and mentorship and will stay current with innovations in data engineering so that they may be evaluated for implementation. This position offers the opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing, science-driven organization making a meaningful difference to patients worldwide.
[...]
Discover your role:• Work with a cross-functional team to optimize and implement our data strategy with a focus on optimization for digital transformation and the use of AI/ML• Design and document end-to-end data architectures that support diverse analytic, operational, and research needs.• Facilitate the implementation of a modern data platform (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks, etc.)• Identify opportunities and implement solutions to increase data interoperability and standardization among systems and across other business units.• Develop and implement pipelines to monitor and improve both internal and external (i.e., from CRO partners) data quality.• Work with informatics and AI engineers optimizing the utility of data for their respective pipelines.• Monitor and optimize the performance of data architectures and platforms.• Develop or implement critical metrics to measure the impact of the overall data strategy.• Stay up to date with the latest advances in the field and, as appropriate, evaluate them for adoption
[...]
This role requires:An advanced degree in computer science, statistics, biomedical informatics, or a related field is preferred (PhD + 2 years of experience or an MS + 4 years of relevant experience). A minimum of 5 years’ experience developing and leading the implementation of data engineering solutions, including accountability for the success of the implementation, in life sciences or healthcare• Demonstrated expertise in designing and maintaining infrastructure and architecture for clinical or biomedical data in a healthcare or life sciences setting.• Expertise in modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, Databricks) and programming languages such as Python, SQL, R, etc.• Maintain and manage code repositories (e.g Bitbucket) ensuring clean, well-documented code with proper version control.• Proficiency in cloud architecture (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP) and DevOps practices. Recognized certifications are a plus.• Experience building, scaling, and maintaining pipelines for structured and unstructured data. Ability to integrate pipelines across the enterprise is essential.• Deep understanding of regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, GDPR, 21 CFR Part 11) and clinical data standards (CDISC, HL7, FHIR).• Knowledge of machine learning pipelines and integration with clinical data platforms.• May require travel up to 20%.

highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) - Taipei | OpenTalent

All jobs
onsite
# Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)
Appier is seeking a Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents) to join their Enterprise Solution Science Team. In this role, you will be responsible for the end-to-end development, from problem definition to model deployment, of cutting-edge AI agents for marketing, strategy, sales, and service applications. You will collaborate with product and ML engineering teams, conduct evaluations, and stay updated with the latest research in LLM and agentic AI.
## About the role
## About the Role
We are looking for a **Machine Learning Scientist (LLM & Agents)** to join the Enterprise Solution Science Team. This team focuses on applying cutting-edge ML technologies to real-world marketing problems by combining them with omnichannel customer data. In this role, you will be responsible for end-to-end development, from problem definition to driving model deployment into production.
## What You’ll Work On
* You will contribute to the design, development, and productionization of one or more enterprise AI agents, including:
* **Marketing Agent**: Empower marketers to improve conversion rates and drive revenue growth.
* **Strategy Agent**: Transform raw data into actionable, revenue-driving strategies, and high-potential audience segments.
* **Sales and Service Agent**: Drive sales conversion, increase average order value (AOV), deepen brand engagement and customer loyalty through real-time, 24/7 AI-powered support.
* Collaborate with product teams to translate product requirements into well-defined machine learning problems
* Work with ML engineers to deploy models into production environments
* Conduct offline and online evaluation, monitoring, and continuous optimization after deployment
* Stay up to date with the latest research and proactively propose innovative applications## What We’re Looking For (Minimum)
* Master’s or PhD degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, or related fields
* Experience in **machine learning**, **LLM**, or **software engineering** through research, internships, projects, or industry work
* Experience or strong interest in **LLM / agentic application development**, including:
* Skills / tools / function calling
* AI Protocol (MCP / A2A)
* Workflow orchestration
* Benchmark evaluation
* Strong mindset with analytical and problem-solving skills, and passion for learning cutting-edge technologies
* Familiarity with AI-powered development tools such as: **Claude Code**, **OpenAI Codex / Codex CLI**, **Cursor**, **Windsurf**, **GitHub Copilot**## What We’re Looking For (Preferred)
* Strong focus on quantitative metrics and systematic evaluation
* Industry experience in MarTech and passion for building customer-centric products
* Familiarity with agentic product development concepts such as:
* Context engineering
* Harness engineering
* Evaluation / testing workflows
* Human-in-the-loop workflows

- Experience designing machine learning operations (MLOps) approaches for model training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement in production environments.
[...]
- Strong data architecture knowledge (data lakes/warehouses, streaming, feature stores, vector databases) and ability to design robust data flows for AI use cases.
[...]
- Experience delivering solutions in life insurance and/or retirement (industry value chain, data considerations, and regulatory constraints).
[...]
- Exposure to emerging enterprise AI patterns such as agentic AI , AI copilots , and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) .
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
[...]
- Strong familiarity with enterprise security, privacy, and governance expectations for AI solutions.
[...]
- Track record of building reusable accelerators, best practices, and reference architectures that improve delivery speed and quality.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.
[...]
As a Machine Learning Scientist:
[...]
Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.
[...]
BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field with 2+ years of relevant work experience. Or Ph. D. with 0-2 years relevant work experience.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including post-training techniques to enhance domain knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Strong software engineering skills and experience working with high-performance computing systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data).
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]

Applied AI ML Engineer-Vice President
[...]
The Corporate & Investment Bank is a global leader across investment banking, wholesale payments, markets and securities services. The world's most important corporations, governments and institutions entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. We provide strategic advice, raise capital, manage risk and extend liquidity in markets around the world. As an Applied AI ML Engineer-Vice President in our Corporate & Investment Bank, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and deploying cutting-edge AI and machine learning solutions to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of our operations. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities for automation and process improvement, utilizing your expertise in machine learning and generative AI.Job Responsibilities
[...]
- Develop and implement machine learning models and algorithms to solve complex operational challenges.
[...]
- Design and deploy generative AI applications to automate and optimize business processes.
[...]
- Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into technical solutions.
[...]
- Analyze large datasets to extract actionable insights and drive data-driven decision-making.
[...]
- Ensure the scalability and reliability of AI/ML solutions in a production environment.
[...]
- Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in AI/ML technologies and integrate them into our operations.
- Mentor and guide junior team members in AI/ML best practices and methodologies.
[...]
Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills
[...]
- Ph.D. with 2+ years experience or Masters with 4+ years experience in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related field.
- Experience in deploying AI/ML applications in a production environment, with skills in deploying models on AWS platforms such as SageMaker or Bedrock.
- Familiarity with MLOps practices, encompassing the full cycle from design, experimentation, deployment, to monitoring and maintenance of machine learning models.
[...]
- Expertise in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Pytorch lightening, or Scikit-learn.
[...]
- Proficiency in programming languages such as Python
[...]
- Proficiency in writing comprehensive test cases, with a strong emphasis on using testing frameworks such as pytest to ensure code quality and reliability.
[...]
- Experience with generative AI models, including GANs, VAEs, or transformers.
[...]
- Solid understanding of data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model evaluation techniques.
[...]
- Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS) and containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes, Amazon EKS).
[...]
- Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.
[...]
Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills
[...]
- Experience in the financial services industry, particularly within investment banking operations.
- Experience in developing AI solutions using agentic frameworks.
- Experience fine-tuning SLMs with approaches like LoRA, QLoRA and DoRA.
- Experience with prompt optimization frameworks such as AutoPrompt and DSPY to enhance the performance and effectiveness of prompt engineering.
- Familiarity with distributed computing systems, frameworks and techniques like data sharding and DDP training
- Experience with Diffusion models is a plus.

results.title: Applied Data Scientist | Hirequorum
highlights:
Applied Data Scientist | Hirequorum
[...]
# Applied Data Scientist
[...]
Are you passionate about using data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics to propel decisions? If you are interested in driving meaningful change for a growth focused consumer products brand, this is an opportunity to join the market-leading Delta Faucet Company team as we use analytics to turbocharge strategic insights!
[...]
Reporting to the Manager of Data Science and Analytics, this role will work with analytics and domain specialists across the business to deliver high-impact insights and data science solutions. You will develop advanced analytical models, experimentation tests, applied AI techniques, and partner with business stakeholders to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that support the company’s growth initiatives.
[...]
As an Applied Data Scientist within Delta Faucet Company’s Analytics Center of Excellence (ACoE), you will act as an owner of high impact analysis and data science initiatives that support complex, ambiguous business decisions. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.
[...]
- Independently lead end-to-end analytics and data science projects, from problem framing through delivery of actionable insights.
- Apply statistical methods, machine learning, and analytical modeling to solve business problems related to growth, customer behavior, market dynamics, and operational efficiency.
- Develop and maintain analytical data models and curated datasets, partnering with data engineering and BI teams.
- Build AI and machine learning solutions that are aligned with business needs.
- Collaborate closely with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into clear recommendations.
- Present data-driven recommendations to leaders across the organization, including tactical and C-suite.
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, or applied machine learning roles.
- 4+ years of hands-on experience applying statistical methods and analytical modeling to real business problems.
- 4+ years of experience using Python for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation.
- 4+ years of experience writing and refining complex SQL queries against large, structured datasets.
- 3+ years of experience partnering directly with business stakeholders to translate analytical findings into actionable recommendations.
- 2+ years of experience working in a modern analytics platform (e.g., Databricks, Spark/PySpark, or similar).
- 1+ years of hands-on‑ experience applying generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM APIs, task‑specific GenAI workflows) in an enterprise analytics environment.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Data Science, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
[...]
- 2+ years of experience with analytics engineering and data modeling, including building reusable, well-documented analytical datasets; familiarity with medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) preferred.
- 1+ years of experience with model monitoring, experiment tracking, and model evaluation in production settings.
- 1+ years of experience with conjoint analysis or other stated-preference research methods.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve complex problems in
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning
